label: WF5 — Broader Stacking Vision
title: Every template from WF3, every artifact from WF1 and WF2—they all become knowledge your assistant can draw on. This is how single-purpose tools become systems.
subtitle: Capability Stacking

label: WF5 — Multi-Context Continuity
title: One assistant, many threads. Each conversation is a different work stream—resume optimization, job evaluation, offer comparison—all drawing from the same knowledge base.
subtitle: Orchestration

label: WF5 — Results and Documentation
title: WF_5a: Tailored application materials. WF_5b: Multi-job evaluation matrix. WF_5c: Offer comparison framework. These are your deliverables—grounded in your perspective, produced through orchestration.
subtitle: Deliverables

label: WF5 — From Tool to System
title: You started with a blank chat window. Now you have an orchestrated system that knows your priorities, holds your context, and compounds in value with every interaction. That's the Rule of 100.
subtitle: Course Complete
